options: Go jump in a lake 三、綜合測驗 ( 第 19-32 題,每題 2 分,共 28 分 ) 說明:以下三篇短文,共有 15 個空格,為第 19 至 32 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案 。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 19 至 23 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 The tooth fairy is a story about a mythical figure that visits kids on the night when they lose their baby teeth. As the kids are sleeping, the tooth fairy takes the tooth 19 money left under the pillow. th The story derived from an 18 century fairy tale, 20 a mouse turned into a fairy and tormented an evil king by hiding under his pillow and knocking out all his teeth. In some European countries, a tooth mouse, 21 a tooth fairy, is believed to visit and actually purchase the teeth from kids. Most cultures 22 some sort of tradition when speaking of their baby teeth. For instance, in Japan, children must throw lower teeth straight up and upper teeth straight down so that their new teeth will be as straight as possible. In the Middle East, teeth are thrown toward the sun for strength. Tooth fairy traditions differ from country to country, but each tries to provide children 23 comfort for the loss of their baby teeth.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: As a consumer, what can we do to stop food fraud from happening again?
options: We should eat less meat to protect our health.
Both the government and individuals are obliged to supervise the companies with food safety issues.
We should give the companies committing food fraud before more opportunities.

Manufacturers with good reputation should sell their products at higher prices. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-40 題。 You probably have heard of The Big Apple, which is a popular nickname of New York. But have you heard of The Big Onion? Few people know it is the nickname for America’s third most populous city, Chicago. The name comes from the Native American word that originally meant “wild onion.” However, you won’t find any wild onions in Chicago downtown. Instead, you might feel the strong wind blowing off Lake Michigan. That’s why Chicago is now nicknamed “the Windy City” by most people. Chicago was incorporated as a city in 1837 and experienced rapid economic growth in the mid-nineteenth century. However, in 1871, the Great Chicago Fire broke out, destroying a large part of the city. During its rebuilding period, Chicago constructed the world’s first skyscraper in 1885, using steel – skeleton construction. Since then, more and more tall buildings have been built. Nowadays Chicago is known for its spectacular skyline. Take Willis Tower, the tallest building in Chicago, for example. At the time of its completion in 1973, it was the tallest building in the world, surpassing the World Trade Center in New York, and it had held the record for 25 years. Now it is the second tallest building in the United States and eighth tallest building in the world. The observation deck is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Chicago and more than one million people visit it every year. In addition to enjoying the city view from above, you can also enjoy great art in the Art Institute of Chicago, where you can enjoy over 50 tours of the collections. If you are a fanatical sports fan, and never want to miss out any games, you can participate in the basketball games in United Center, and baseball games in Wrigley Field and Cellular Field as well. If all the exploring makes you hungry, try the most famous Chicago hot dog with mustard, tomatoes, hot peppers and more. Or you can grab a bite at the most delicious popcorn store Garret Popcorn. With so much to see, do, and eat, how can you resist the charm of Chicago?

options: You can enjoy the city view from the top of the Art Institute of Chicago.

No other building in Chicago is taller than Willis Tower. 第貳部分:非選擇題 ( 第 1 至 5 題,每題 4 分,共 20 分 ) 一、填充 說明:請依據中文之提示,在空格內填入適當之詞彙以完成句子。每格限填一字,並將答案寫在 「答案卷」之指定範圍內。
